text: While research-policy dialogues are well-established at the national and EU levels, they are increasingly taking place locally within European cities. Despite this trend, there is surprisingly little formal analysis of how municipal policymakers engage with researchers or how scholars attempt to shape urban policy. This chapter explores the diverse forms, thematic content, and practical applications of research-policy dialogues in European cities. It further examines how these local interactions are influenced by national policy frameworks and the growing role of the European Union in facilitating knowledge transfer across borders. The study concludes that the EU has become a pivotal force in promoting a horizontal exchange of "best practices" by providing dedicated research funding and fostering robust networks of European cities, effectively creating a new layer of knowledge-sharing that bypasses traditional national constraints.
